description: SBS corridor from Woodside (near LIRR) to Rockaway Beach, passing through Elmhurst, Rego Park, Middle Village, Woodhaven, Ozone Park, Howard Beach, and Rockaway Park.

Corridor for proposed Select Bus Service, including Woodhaven Boulevard and Cross Bay Boulevard
Study corridor from north to south Queens along Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards
14-mile long SBS corridor from Woodside to the Rockaways, based on Q52/53 LTD bus route, 30,000 daily bus riders

Corridor heavily used by bus riders, drivers, and pedestrians. Roughly 400,000 residents live within a 15-minute walk. 10 bus routes operate along the corridor. Connects to A train at Liberty Av, J/Z at Jamaica, and M/R at Queens Blvd.
14-mile corridor along Woodhaven Blvd and Cross Bay Blvd from Woodside to the Rockaways

Based on the existing Q52/53 LTD bus route, 14 miles long from Woodside to the Rockaways. Within a 15-minute walk of the corridor: 400,000 residents, 43% of households do not own a car, 60% of residents commute by transit.
Cross Bay Boulevard corridor for proposed Q52/Q53 Select Bus Service
Corridor studied for Select Bus Service implementation, described as one of the most dangerous corridors in the city for drivers and pedestrians.

14 mile corridor along Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards
Total SBS corridor spanning Woodhaven Blvd and Cross Bay Blvd. 30,000+ daily bus riders. 400,000 residents within 15-min walk. Vision Zero Priority Corridor since 2009.

Corridor for proposed Q52/Q53 Select Bus Service in Queens, New York

Woodhaven / Cross Bay Blvds are wide thoroughfares unlike others in the city that present a unique opportunity for innovative service and design. They create critical connections both locally and to the Rockaways.
Corridor from Jackson Heights to Rockaway Park via Woodhaven Blvd and Cross Bay Blvd
14 miles long from Woodside to the Rockaways, based on the existing Q52/53 LTD bus route; 30,000 daily bus riders

14 miles from Woodside to the Rockaways along Woodhaven Boulevard and Cross Bay Boulevard

One of the only direct north-south roadways in Queens, providing critical connections for bus riders and drivers
Important north/south transit corridor for Queens; provides connections to 8 subway lines, over 20 bus routes, and the LIRR
Important north/south transit corridor carrying over 30,000 daily bus riders in Queens. Vision Zero Priority Corridor between Queens Blvd and 165 Ave.
Important north/south transit corridor for Queens. Provides connections to 8 subway lines, 20+ bus routes, and LIRR. Based on the existing Q52/53 Limited bus route.

records.display_name: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevards Select Bus Service

description: Long-term SBS project on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards addressing bus speed, pedestrian safety, and traffic flow. Includes 2014 bus lane implementation and future capital project for full SBS.
Planning and implementation of Select Bus Service along Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards corridor, including short-term bus lane improvements starting fall 2014 and long-term SBS planning
Conceptual design phase for Select Bus Service on Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevards, with three concepts under discussion: Offset Bus Lanes, Main Road Bus Lanes, and Median Busway
Select Bus Service on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards based on the existing Q52/Q53 bus routes
Third public meeting for the Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards SBS project took place November 5, 2014. Three design concepts presented: offset bus lanes, main road bus lanes, and median bus lanes.
Phase II SBS corridor project on Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevard, identified as priority transit corridor in BRT Phase II Plan
Proposed Select Bus Service for the Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevard area serving Q52 and Q53 routes

Proposed SBS route on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards based on the Q52/53 LTD bus route, from Woodside to the Rockaways. Includes main road bus lanes, median stations, offset bus lanes on Cross Bay Blvd, off-board fare collection, and other transit priority treatments.

Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street where buses operate quickly and reliably, bus customers safely access stations, pedestrians are comfortable, and drivers travel at reasonable and safe speeds. Community Advisory Committee Meeting #3.
Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street with main road bus lanes, median stations, offset bus lanes, and bus bulbs
Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street with Select Bus Service, bus lanes, median stations, and pedestrian safety improvements
Public Design Workshop #2 discussion materials presenting proposed SBS routes for Q52 and Q53 on Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevard

Planning for long-term Select Bus Service along the Woodhaven/Cross Bay Blvd corridor, including a community input process with Community Advisory Committee, public workshops, and meetings with Community Boards and stakeholders.
Proposed SBS along Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards spanning 14 miles from Woodside to the Rockaways
Proposed Select Bus Service (SBS) for Q52 and Q53 routes on Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevard, including proposed new stops, local stop discontinuations, and stop consolidation

Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street where buses operate quickly and reliably, bus customers safely access stations, pedestrians are comfortable, and drivers go at reasonable safe speeds

Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards Select Bus Service (SBS) route based on existing Q52 and Q53 bus routes. Important north/south transit corridor carrying over 30,000 daily bus riders in Queens. Project goal to transform corridor into a complete street with faster/more reliable bus service, safer streets, and improved traffic and local conditions. Proposed as short-term Operational Project in 2017 followed by longer-term Capital Project.
Draft plans for Woodhaven Boulevard between Union Turnpike and Rockaway Boulevard
Draft plans for Woodhaven Boulevard between Queens Boulevard and Union Turnpike
Draft plans for Cross Bay Boulevard between Rockaway Boulevard and 165 Avenue presented at Public Design Workshop #3
Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street with SBS, bus lanes, transit signal priority, pedestrian safety improvements, and median bus stops
SBS corridor project based on existing Q52/53 LTD bus route, 14 miles from Woodside to Rockaways

Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street where buses operate quickly and reliably, bus customers safely access stations, pedestrians are comfortable walking, and drivers get where they need to go at a reasonable and safe speed
Proposed SBS corridor along Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards based on existing Q52/53 LTD route, with dedicated bus lanes, median stations, off-board fare collection, and TSP

Long-term project to implement Select Bus Service along Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards, including bus lanes, enhanced stations, bus signal priority, branding, improved fare collection, and passenger information
SBS project will look at all potential changes, focusing on faster bus service, safer streets, and maintaining appropriate traffic flow
Proposed Select Bus Service (SBS) routes for Q52 and Q53 along Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevard corridor in Woodhaven, Queens
Convert Q52/Q53 to Select Bus Service and transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into complete streets with bus lanes, median stations, pedestrian safety treatments, and signal optimization

Based on the existing Q52/53 Limited bus route. 14 miles long from Woodside to the Rockaways. Important north/south transit corridor for Queens. Provides connections to 8 subway lines, 20+ bus routes, and LIRR. DOT and MTA committed to delivering Woodhaven / Cross Bay Blvd (Q52/Q53) SBS.

Transform Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street with faster/more reliable bus service, safer streets for all users, and improved traffic and local conditions. Based on existing Q52/Q53 bus routes. Phased approach: short-term project in 2017 with SBS launch, followed by longer-term capital project.
SBS project on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards based on the existing Q52/53 LTD bus route, 14 miles long from Woodside to the Rockaways

Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street with bus lanes, median SBS stations, and traffic safety improvements. Based on the existing Q52/53 Limited bus route. 30,000+ daily bus riders. 14 miles from Woodside to the Rockaways.
Joint NYCDOT and MTA project to implement Select Bus Service on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards with bus lanes, off-board fare payment, transit signal priority, and pedestrian safety improvements
Select Bus Service project on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards serving the Q52 and Q53 bus routes
Draft street design plans for Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards between Queens Boulevard and 165 Avenue

Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street with dedicated bus lanes, median bus stops, transit signal priority, off-board fare collection, and pedestrian safety improvements. Phased as a 2017 implementation project followed by a capital project.
Phased SBS project along Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards bringing high-quality transit service, bus lanes, median bus stations, off-board fare collection, TSP, and pedestrian safety improvements.
Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street with SBS service
